leadText: Pastoria City (Japanese: ノモセシティ Nomose City) is a city in the Sinnoh region. It is also the home of the Move Reminder, the Scarf Guy and the Sinnoh region's Safari Zone. It is a city on the edge of a giant swamp, known as the Great Marsh. The marsh was once part of the sea, which drained away over time leaving the marshland. There are rare Pokémon only found in the swamps. The Great Marsh keeps the weather cool in the summertime, making it an ideal town for Water Pokémon. Croagunk is one of the rare Pokémon that can be found in the swamp.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The mascot of Pastoria City is Croagunk , as there is a statue of one in the Poké Mart. This is reflected in the Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl episode Cream of the Croagunk Crop! , when the city holds a Croagunk Festival in honor of their mascot. Southeast of the city, there are two ships, but they have no known use for transportation. In Platinum, if the player stands behind the Croagunk Wall with the player's head sticking out of it, a wild Croagunk will appear, cry out, and walk into the Great Marsh building. Standing right outside the Move Reminder's house is a man who claims to be standing "smack-dab in the middle of town." However, according to the map, he is placed four steps down and five steps to the right of the true center of town. Upon the player's arrival in Pastoria City in Diamond and Pearl, a Team Galactic Grunt refers to them as a "little bugger". This line was changed to say "little rascal" in the European release and Platinum and "little twerp " in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, as "bugger" is an expletive in some parts of the English-speaking world. Origin [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Pokémon world in relation to the real world → Sinnoh Pastoria City may correspond to Kushiro , the capital city of Kushiro Subprefecture in Hokkaido . The northeast of the city is bordered by the Kushiro-shitsugen National Park , the largest wetlands in Japan.
